nodes.label: What-If Curiosity Prompt
nodes.rationale: The starting point of exploration â€” a naturally-stated curiosity ('What would happen if...' or 'I wonder whether...'). Capturing the origin of curiosity helps identify which kinds of intuitions tend to be productive.

nodes.label: Written Expectations Before Sprint
nodes.rationale: Without written, specific expectations you cannot recognize surprise â€” and surprise is what turns exploration into research. Vague expectations cannot be violated. The template requires explicit articulation of what would genuinely surprise the researcher (1-2 outcomes).

nodes.label: Fruitfulness Pre-Check
nodes.rationale: Before investing sprint time, check whether the best possible outcome would actually lead somewhere worth going. Questions: (1) If this surprises you, then what downstream consequences follow? (2) Who would care â€” can you name a specific community or researcher? (3) Does this connect to LossFunk research directions? Strategically pointless explorations may be intellectually fun but waste lab time.

nodes.label: Time-Box Discipline
nodes.rationale: Sprints are strictly time-boxed (3 days, 1 week, or 2 weeks) with a check-in date and a hard stop date. At check-in: if nothing surprising is emerging, consider stopping early. No extensions without a new sprint. Forces a decision (Graduate/Shelve/Pivot) rather than allowing exploration to drift.

nodes.label: Graduate / Shelve / Pivot Decision
nodes.rationale: Three outcomes: GRADUATE (surprise found â€” 'I expected X but observed Y' â€” pull out the Research Question Sharpener); SHELVE (no surprise found, expectations confirmed or ambiguous â€” file notes, no shame, most sprints end here); PIVOT (original what-if wasn't fruitful but something adjacent is â€” start a new sprint). Most sprints ending in 'shelve' is explicitly framed as success, not failure.

nodes.label: LossFunk Research Flow
nodes.rationale: Formal pipeline: Curiosity ('what if...') â†’ Exploration Sprint (time-boxed) â†’ Surprise found? Yes â†’ Research Question Sharpener â†’ Project. No â†’ Shelve or Pivot to new sprint. Exploration is search, not commitment.

edges.source_location: page 2, Does this connect to our research directions?
